topic: LLM Gateway Comparison 2026: Portkey vs Helicone vs LiteLLM

competitors.title: Best Helicone Alternative: Portkey, Langfuse, Braintrust Compared
key_points: Portkey supports 200+ LLM providers with routing, fallback, and semantic caching capabilities
Recommends Portkey as primary Helicone migration option for multi-provider gateway needs
Decision framework based on use case: gateway vs observability vs evals
Stockyard highlighted for self-hosted single-binary deployment as alternative

competitors.title: Best LLM Routers in 2026: ClawRouters, OpenRouter, LiteLLM, Bifrost, Portkey
key_points: Reviews 11 best LLM routers in 2026 including Portkey and LiteLLM
Save up to Tenth on AI costs with the right router choice
Compares latency overhead: Bifrost at 11μs (Rust-based) vs. LiteLLM Python bottleneck at 500 RPS
ClawRouters: free BYOK model, AI-powered task classification for cost optimization

competitors.title: LLM Proxy Landscape 2026: Helicone Acquired, LiteLLM Compromised, What's Next
key_points: Covers both Helicone acquisition and LiteLLM compromise in context
LLM proxy landscape shake-up creating migration urgency
Stockyard as self-hosted alternative to both tools
LiteLLM security compromise in 2026 (alongside Helicone acquisition) created dual migration pressure

competitors.title: Langfuse vs LangSmith vs Braintrust vs Helicone 2026 Comparison
key_points: Definitive comparison of top four platforms including Helicone
Pricing at scale: Langfuse $919/month vs LangSmith $2,500+ at 1M traces
Self-hosted Langfuse drops to ~$150/month in infrastructure costs
Feature matrix across tracing, evals, prompt management, CI/CD

stats.fact: Helicone acquired by Mintlify on March 3, 2026; team joined Mintlify in San Francisco
Helicone is in maintenance mode: only security patches, bug fixes, and new model support — no new features
LiteLLM security compromise in 2026 (alongside Helicone acquisition) created dual migration pressure in LLM proxy market
Portkey supports 200+ LLM providers with routing, fallback, and semantic caching capabilities
Bifrost LLM gateway adds only 11 microseconds (0.011ms) overhead at 5,000 RPS — negligible latency impact
Simple queries cost ~$0.10/M tokens; complex coding tasks cost ~$30/M tokens — 300x price gap between model tiers
Smart routing combined with caching achieves 47–80% cost reduction in production AI systems
RouteLLM (UC Berkeley) achieves 85% cost reduction on MT Bench while maintaining 95% of GPT-4 performance quality
Enterprise LLM API spending grew from $3.5B (late 2024) to $8.4B (mid-2025), 2.4x in ~6 months
37% of enterprises use 5 or more LLMs in production environments in 2026

angles: Timing angle: 2026 is the perfect time for LLM gateway comparison with Helicone acquired and LiteLLM compromised
Migration urgency: Teams currently on Helicone or LiteLLM need to evaluate alternatives like Portkey
Cost optimization: Gateway routing can achieve 85% cost savings with proper model selection
Architecture comparison: Proxy-based (Helicone) vs SDK-based (Langfuse) vs gateway-based (Portkey) approaches
Security considerations: Choosing between commercial (Portkey) vs open-source (LiteLLM) with different risk profiles
Enterprise readiness: Portkey positioned as enterprise-grade vs LiteLLM's community focus
Future-proofing: Choosing a gateway that supports evolving LLM provider landscape (200+ in Portkey vs 100+ in LiteLLM)
